title: 19.-Contract No. 32400029 Amendment: Baldoni Construction Services, Inc. for Echo Lake Camp Accessibility Upgrades

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution increasing the not to exceed amount for Contract No. 32400029 with
Baldoni Construction Service, Inc. for the Echo Lake Camp Accessibility Upgrades
Project by $60,000 for an amended total amount not to exceed $616,953.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for the $60,000 amendment is available in the FY2025 General Fund (Fund
011) budget. No other funding is required, and no other projects will be delayed due to
this expenditure.
sections.current_situation:
On May 23, 2023, City Council authorized a not-to-exceed total for the Baldoni
Construction Service, Inc. contract for the Echo Lake Camp Accessibility Upgrades of
$436,954 which included a 20% contingency (Resolution No. 70,855-N.S.). The
contract with Baldoni Construction Service, Inc. was executed on August 28, 2023 for
an amount of $364,128. On September 10, 2024, City Council authorized an increased
not-to-exceed total for the Baldoni Construction Service, Inc. contract of $556,953
(Resolution No. 71,500-N.S.). Two contract amendments have been executed to date
to address unanticipated existing conditions and conflicts and emergency work,
resulting in a current total contract value of $552,862. In April, 2025, the need to
address asphalt failure at the Echo Lake sports court due to root intrusion was
identified. Because the scope of additional work is similar to the work Baldoni is
currently completing for the City, the City proposes to complete this additional work via
change order with Baldoni Construction, Inc. The contract authority needs to be
increased by $60,000 to allow this work to proceed.
sections.background:
The Echo Lake Camp Accessibility Upgrade Project was advertised for bids on March 6,
2023, and bids were opened on Tuesday, April 19, 2023. The City received three bids.
Baldoni Construction was the lowest responsive and responsible bidder. Staff
conducted reference checks and received satisfactory feedback, and recommended
that a contract for this project be awarded to Baldoni Construction. The contract was
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website: http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/manager
Page 2 of 4
Contract: Baldoni Construction CONSENT CALENDAR
Echo Lake Camp Accessibility Upgrade Project May 20, 2025
executed on August 28, 2023, and work began in September, 2023. Baldoni has
completed the accessible pathway improvements, however unanticipated conditions
required a revision to the original approach to provide accessible parking. During 2023-
2024, the City developed an alternative accessible parking approach. Change Order
#1, reflecting the changed approach to accessible parking, was completed in June,
2024. The pathway installation was completed in Fall, 2024.
Berkeley Tuolumne Camp reconstruction was completed in 2022. Throughout the
Camp, the City used pervious geo-grid in lieu of asphalt at parking lots and driveways to
reduce impervious surfaces at Camp and protect water quality. During the 2024
summer season, two sections of geo-grid material failed at the steepest sections of
driveway, including undermining of an accessible crosswalk. Echo Lake Camp relies on
electrical power to pump, treat, and distribute drinking water for the Camp, as well as to
pump and treat wastewater. In recent years, power shut offs by the local utility provider
for wildfire risk mitigation has significantly increased the occurrence and duration of
power outages. Change Order #2 was completed in November, 2024 to address these
needs. The Berkeley Tuolumne Camp driveway was com
sections.rationale:
Echo Lake has been providing valuable outdoor activities to Berkeley youth and families
since 1922. Repair of the failing sports court and relocation of the basketball hoop post
will allow these facilities to continue to provide safe programming at this unique City
resource.
sections.alternatives:
None

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO 32400029 AMENDMENT: BALDONI CONSTRUCTION SERVICE,
INC., FOR ECHO LAKE CAMP ACCESSIBILITY UPGRADES
WHEREAS, the City has owned the Echo Lake Camp since 1922; and
WHEREAS, the City wants to voluntarily improve accessibility at Echo Lake Camp; and
WHEREAS, an invitation for bids was duly advertised and three bids were opened on
April 18, 2023, and Baldoni Construction Service, Inc. was the lowest responsive and
responsible bidder; and
WHEREAS, the City Council authorized execution of a contract and any amendments,
extension and/or change orders until completion of the Project with Baldoni Construction
Service, Inc. in an amount not to exceed $436,954 (Resolution No. 70,855-N.S); and
WHEREAS, two contract amendments have been executed to date to address
unanticipated existing conditions and conflicts and emergency work, resulting in a
current total contract value of $552,862; and
WHEREAS, the sports court at Echo Lake Camp is failing due to root intrusion
additional funds are needed to complete repairs; and
WHEREAS, funding for the $60,000 amendment is available in the FY2025 General
Fund (Fund 011) budget.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
not to exceed amount for Contract No. 32400029 with Baldoni Construction Service,
Inc. for the Echo Lake Camp Accessibility Upgrade Project is increased by $60,000 for
an amended total not to exceed $616,953. A record signature of said agreements and
any amendments to be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
